author: @richardlord5573
text: If you drilll a hole in your container 3/4 of  the way up then a 1/4 of the water drains out and goes somewhere.   Let's say that somewhere is another vessel.  Are you suggesting that the other vessel is outside the universe, which it can't be because the fact that water can drain from one to the other means they are part of the same universe (system).  Ultimately you still have the same volume of water (4 lumps) but you have just split them.

author: @youcanthandlethetruth6976
text: In Modern Physics, people like Newton and Einstein would calculate 0 into almost anything. Einstein calculated it as his singularity and equated it to infinity. He represented his models as closed systems, but actually if you follow his equations, his models were open systems and that caused a whole heap of confusion within the Physics realm. Because in Einstein's models, he took everything out of the Universe, except a black hole or two, which had zero mass and thus zero energy, but actually gave it an infinite potential of energy. Well if you actually abide by his perspective, then it begs the question, if this universe has no mass and thus no energy, how are singularities infinite energy zones? Well it must be a pathway to another Universe where it can pull energy from outside of itself, otherwise it's complete hogwash.

author: @freeenergynews
text: Good example remind me that physics always calculates with closed systems and thus prohibits free energy systems, that need open systems...

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: freeenergynews good point mate - i hadn't thought of that

author: @stevesimpson5994
text:
There is the very familiar case of 12 + 1 = 1, which makes perfect sense in the universe of clocks (and modulo arithmetic), but seems strange otherwise.

So, if I understand your intent, "Apply maths (and science in general), when and only if it makes sense."

Keep up the good work :-)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Steve Simpson I laughed out loud about that one mate - very clever and good point - and yes that is what i meant

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Florin Nichifiriuc lol - superb mate - and very nicely put

author: @erlendse
text:
Kinda bad examples, with clipped scale.

The effect of 1+1 may not be 2, stuff isn't always that simple.

And for absolute accuracy, how do you pick up exactly one? And is evry ones the same size?

But they do get math quite accurate, but the world doesn't follow math. (Not our at least)

Same with laws of nature, more like observations with few exceptions from the model.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: erlendse sometimes more than a few mate

replies.text: BRITISH YOUTUBER Christopher E cheers mate

author: @MartinPHellwig
text: So what I get from this is that our universe is only coherent to the mathematics we use because the universe as it works defines what our understanding of mathematics is. In other words, just be careful that you apply the correct mathematics to the correct universe and don't exclude possibilities just because they aren't calculable. Rather philosophically :-)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Martin P. Hellwig absolutely mate - and yes a bit - but our understanding of things often dictates what we are capable of looking at

author: @SuperFinGuy
text: But if drill a hole at level 3 you're subtracting the water, the result is 3 because you subtracted 1 somewhere.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: ***** you might be missing the point mate - i haven't subtracted one - i have changed the universe the maths operates in - that's the point - have a go at thinking up other examples - roland did

replies.author: @SuperFinGuy
replies.text: Robert Murray-Smith I'd say you've changed the equation but not the math rules.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: ***** i see - well - i'd say we have kept the rules but changed what we apply them to - have a look at diophantine equations for a this universe example of what i am talking about

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: ***** another thing to think about is the proof of pythagoras i did - think about why it is true and then why it is untrue - because it is both - depending on the universe you look at
